publications.title: 2938: Atlantis
publications.text: A science fiction novella set in a future where Earth faces collapse from a devastating virus. Following humanity’s desperate escape to the starship Atlantis, the story explores survival, trust, and the fragile line between hope and horror.

publications.title: At the Creative Writing of Madness
publications.text: A Zillow Wirral article on cosmic horror, Lovecraftian fiction, incomprehensible beings, and how reading difficult horror helped build vocabulary and confidence in writing.

publications.title: The Gen Z Stare: A Cultural & Psychological Phenomenon
publications.text: A paper defining the Gen Z stare as a prolonged, neutral, wide-eyed gaze with minimal affect in contexts where expressive response is socially expected.

publications.title: The Half-Silvering Framework
publications.text: A collection of works on asymmetric observation, layered systems, directional definition, formal logic, secure architectures, AI, and biomedical modelling.

links.label: Layered Systems, Secure Architectures and AI

focusCards.title: Observation is directional.
focusCards.text: Higher-order layers can observe or define lower layers. Lower layers cannot define higher layers.

focusCards.title: Observation is neutral.
focusCards.text: Observation means interaction or state-definition. It does not require human awareness or consciousness.

focusCards.title: Self-observation is allowed.
focusCards.text: A layer can register or validate its own state. In the formal matrix, self-observation is marked as true.

works.title: The Half-Silvering Framework for Layered Systems in Secure Architectures and AI
works.text: A research work applying the Half-Silvering Framework to layered systems, secure architectures, and AI.

works.title: Half-Silvering Dimensions: A Formal Expansion of Directional Observation Logic in Layered Systems
works.text: A formal expansion using truth tables, symbolic language, equations, and simulations to test downward definition propagation.

works.title: Beyond Observation: Asymmetric Definability in Theoretical and Biomedical Models
works.source: American Journal of Biomedical Science & Research · 2025
works.text: An opinion article applying asymmetric definability to theoretical and biomedical modelling.

works.title: Half-Silvering Dimensions
works.source: Global Journal of Science Frontier Research · 2024
works.text: The original conceptual paper introducing the Half-Silvering Dimensions model.

experience.text: Represents the Wirral Care-Leavers Council in meetings, collaborating with local authorities and stakeholders to advocate for care leavers and improve community services.
Invited contributor to an academic report on the Staying Close Programme in the Wirral.
Invited guest speaker to the Young People's Action Group Committee, representing the Wirral Care-Leavers Council.
Candidate for the UK Youth Parliament for the Wirral constituency.

awards.title: Gold - Innovation & Creativity
awards.text: National recognition connected to 2938: Atlantis and creative achievement.

artworks.title: Fighting the Jabberwock
artworks.text: A figure stands in confrontation with an unseen force. The fractured mask and radiating darkness represent fear, identity, and the pressure of internal conflict. The work explores what it feels like to face something larger than yourself, even when the battle cannot be clearly defined.

artworks.title: How to Hide a Broken Heart
artworks.text: Layers of pink, blue, and dripping paint create a surface that appears playful at first glance, but unstable underneath. The painting reflects the way emotion is often concealed behind colour, humour, or performance. It is about covering damage without truly removing it.

artworks.title: Temporary
artworks.text: Soft forms emerge and disappear within a dark field of colour. The painting focuses on impermanence, memory, and emotional states that exist only briefly before fading. Nothing in the composition settles completely, mirroring the instability of passing moments.

artworks.title: Down the Rabbit Hole
artworks.text: Small scattered marks pull the viewer into an almost endless visual space. Inspired by the feeling of spiralling thought, the piece represents curiosity, overstimulation, and losing orientation while searching for meaning.

practiceCards.title: Confrontation
practiceCards.text: Figures, masks, pressure, fear, and the feeling of standing against something that cannot be fully named.

practiceCards.title: Concealment
practiceCards.text: Colour, humour, performance, and surface brightness used to cover damage without removing it.

practiceCards.title: Impermanence
practiceCards.text: Forms that appear and disappear, reflecting memory, temporary states, and moments that refuse to settle.

philosophy: Digital legacy and the version of a person left behind by platforms, posts, drafts, messages, and archived traces.
Music and memory, especially how songs become containers for moments that are otherwise difficult to explain.
Perception at night, where the same space can feel altered by darkness, attention, uncertainty, and emotion.
Small moments, writing, ego, identity, and the strange value of thoughts that pass before they become fixed.
writing.identityQuote: The work asks the same kind of question in different forms: what defines a thing, what hides underneath it, and what remains when the surface changes?
